comment: This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tstep
description:
Creates groups of values - buckets - out of a `@timestamp` attribute.
Unlike `TBUCKET`, which aligns buckets at their `start`, `TSTEP` returns bucket `end` timestamps,
anchored to UTC. With `TRANGE`, bucket boundaries advance from the range start.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width. With `TRANGE`, bucket ends are generated from the range start in increments of `step` and considered up to range end. Without `TRANGE`, buckets are anchored on the current query time.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width. With `TRANGE`, bucket ends are generated from the range start in increments of `step` and considered up to range end. Without `TRANGE`, buckets are anchored on the current query time.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| WHERE TRANGE("2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT min

FROM sample_data
| WHERE TRANGE("2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP("1 hour")
| SORT min
